Compliance Obligations

IEC 62304

Rule IDClauseMessageRemediation
IEC62304-5.1.15.1.1software-development-plan: A Software Development Plan must be established and maintainedCreate and maintain a Software Development Plan covering lifecycle activities, deliverables, and traceability
IEC62304-5.1.65.1.6software-verification-plan: A Software Verification Plan must define verification activities and acceptance criteriaDocument verification strategy including unit tests, integration tests, and acceptance criteria
IEC62304-5.2.15.2.1software-requirements-specification: Software requirements must be documented and traceable to system requirementsCreate a Software Requirements Specification (SRS) with traceable requirements
IEC62304-5.3.15.3.1architecture-documentation: Software architecture must be documented showing subdivision into software itemsDocument software architecture with component diagrams and interface descriptions
IEC62304-5.4.15.4.1detailed-design: Detailed design documentation is required for Class C software itemsCreate detailed design documents for each software unit including algorithms and data structures
IEC62304-6.16.1maintenance-plan: A Software Maintenance Plan must be establishedEstablish a maintenance plan covering feedback handling, modification analysis, and release procedures
IEC62304-7.1.17.1.1risk-analysis: Software risk analysis per ISO 14971 must be performed and documentedPerform and document risk analysis in accordance with ISO 14971, including hazard identification and risk control
IEC62304-8.1.18.1.1configuration-management: A Configuration Management system must be established for all software itemsImplement configuration management covering version control, change control, and baseline identification

OWASP Top 10

Rule IDClauseMessageRemediation
OWASP-A04-001A04:2021threat-model: A threat model must be created and maintained for the applicationCreate a threat model document identifying assets, trust boundaries, threat actors, and mitigations (STRIDE or similar methodology)
OWASP-A04-002A04:2021secure-design-review: Security design reviews must be conducted for significant changesEstablish a security design review process for new features, architectural changes, and third-party integrations
OWASP-A06-001A06:2021dependency-management: Dependencies must be tracked and regularly updated for security patchesMaintain a software bill of materials (SBOM), run dependency vulnerability scans (pip-audit, safety), and establish a patching cadence
OWASP-A09-001A09:2021security-logging-policy: Security-relevant events must be logged with sufficient detailDefine a security logging policy covering authentication events, access control failures, input validation failures, and administrative actions
OWASP-A09-002A09:2021incident-response-plan: An incident response plan must be established and testedCreate an incident response plan with detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review procedures

HIPAA

Rule IDClauseMessageRemediation
HIPAA-164.308-a1164.308(a)(1)risk-analysis: A risk analysis must identify threats and vulnerabilities to ePHIConduct and document a comprehensive risk analysis identifying threats, vulnerabilities, and likelihood of impact to ePHI
HIPAA-164.308-a3164.308(a)(3)workforce-security-policy: Policies must ensure workforce members have appropriate access to ePHIDocument workforce security procedures including authorization, supervision, clearance, and termination procedures for ePHI access
HIPAA-164.308-a4164.308(a)(4)information-access-management: Access to ePHI must be managed based on role and minimum necessary standardDocument access authorization policies implementing the minimum necessary standard for ePHI access
HIPAA-164.308-a5164.308(a)(5)security-awareness-training: Security awareness and training program must be established for all workforce membersEstablish a security awareness training program covering password management, log-in monitoring, and malicious software protection
HIPAA-164.308-a6164.308(a)(6)security-incident-procedures: Procedures for identifying, responding to, and mitigating security incidents must be documentedDocument security incident response procedures including identification, response, mitigation, and documentation of incidents and outcomes
HIPAA-164.308-a7164.308(a)(7)contingency-plan: A contingency plan must address data backup, disaster recovery, and emergency operationsDocument contingency plan including data backup plan, disaster recovery plan, emergency mode operation plan, and testing procedures
HIPAA-164.310-a1164.310(a)(1)facility-access-controls: Policies must limit physical access to electronic information systems and facilitiesDocument facility access controls including contingency operations, facility security plan, access control and validation, and maintenance records
HIPAA-164.314-a1164.314(a)(1)business-associate-agreements: Business Associate Agreements must be in place with all entities that handle ePHIEnsure Business Associate Agreements are executed with all vendors, subcontractors, and partners who create, receive, maintain, or transmit ePHI
HIPAA-164.316-b1164.316(b)(1)documentation-retention-policy: HIPAA-required documentation must be retained for at least six yearsEstablish a documentation retention policy ensuring all HIPAA-required policies, procedures, and records are retained for a minimum of six years

SOC2

Rule IDClauseMessageRemediation
SOC2-CC6-003CC6.1access-control-policy: An access control policy must define roles, permissions, and provisioning proceduresDocument access control policies including role definitions, least-privilege principle, access reviews, and deprovisioning procedures
SOC2-CC6-004CC6.3access-review-process: Periodic access reviews must be conducted to verify appropriate permissionsEstablish quarterly access reviews for all systems, documenting reviewer, date, and actions taken
SOC2-CC7-003CC7.2monitoring-and-alerting: System monitoring and alerting must be implemented for security eventsDocument monitoring strategy including alert thresholds, escalation procedures, and on-call rotation
SOC2-CC7-004CC7.3incident-response-plan: An incident response plan must be documented and testedCreate and annually test an incident response plan covering detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery
SOC2-CC8-001CC8.1change-management-policy: A change management policy must govern all production changesDocument change management procedures including change request, approval, testing, deployment, and rollback steps
SOC2-CC9-001CC9.1risk-assessment: Regular risk assessments must identify and evaluate operational and security risksConduct and document annual risk assessments covering operational, security, and compliance risks with mitigation plans
SOC2-A1-001A1.2availability-plan: A business continuity and disaster recovery plan must be documentedDocument BCP/DR plans including RTOs, RPOs, backup procedures, failover testing, and recovery procedures
SOC2-C1-002C1.1data-classification-policy: A data classification policy must define sensitivity levels and handling proceduresDefine data classification levels (public, internal, confidential, restricted) with handling, storage, and disposal requirements for each
SOC2-C1-003C1.2encryption-policy: Encryption standards must be defined for data at rest and in transitDocument encryption requirements specifying algorithms, key lengths, key management, and certificate lifecycle for data at rest and in transit
Guard v1.1.0 | 2026-03-27 13:34 UTC
